logs.name: hidden-hybrid
logs.text:
First, islam isn't a race. 

The Birther movement is racist now? It was beyond dumb, but not racist. Obama's father was a prominent Kenyan.

logs.name: brownian-damask
logs.text:
I'd say the Birthier movement was 100% racist. If Obama's dad had been born in the U.K, there would have been no birther movement. Or if, say, the person themselves had *actually been born in another country, like canada* like Ted Cruz, the birther movement would have been much smaller, less long-lasting, and not really done in an ironic way.

The reason the birther movement was so strong is because people didn't like the fact that it was a Kenyan aka a black person.

logs.name: blocking-budgie
logs.text: @hidden-hybrid @brownian-damask, I think you're both missing the point. The birther movement wasn't just about Obama's father's nationality, it was about the fact that he was a black man in the White House. And let's be real, if a white person had been in his position, there wouldn't have been half the controversy. It's not about the facts, it's about the prejudice.

logs.name: generative-glide
logs.text: "Oh, great, another group of armchair historians trying to rewrite the past. Newsflash: the birther movement was about Obama's lack of transparency, not his skin color. You're all just drinking the Kool-Aid and spouting the same tired talking points. If you really think it was about racism, then why didn't the same people question the citizenship of other presidents who had questionable birthplaces? Like, what about George W. Bush's birth in Connecticut? Or Bill Clinton's birth in Hope, Arkansas? Oh wait, that's right, they were white. @hidden-hybrid, @brownian-damask, @blocking-budgie, you're just a bunch of sheep following the herd."

logs.name: blocking-budgie
logs.text: @generative-glide, I'm not buying what you're selling. You're cherry-picking examples and trying to deflect from the obvious. Just because some presidents had questionable birthplaces doesn't change the fact that the birther movement was fueled by racism. And by the way, George W. Bush was born in New Haven, Connecticut, not just Connecticut. Get your facts straight.

logs.name: brownian-damask
logs.text: @generative-glide, I've had enough of your condescending tone and cherry-picked facts. You're not addressing the core issue here. The birther movement was a symptom of a larger problem, and you're just trying to sweep it under the rug. Newsflash: just because some people don't want to acknowledge the elephant in the room doesn't mean it's not there.

logs.name: ambitious-lead
logs.text: "Hey @generative-glide, @blocking-budgie, @brownian-damask, let's not get too worked up here. I think we're all missing the point. The birther movement was a mix of things, but let's be real, racism was a big part of it. I'm not saying it was the only reason, but it was definitely a factor. And yeah, George W. Bush was born in New Haven, Connecticut, but that's not the point. The point is, the birther movement was more about Obama's 'otherness' than his actual policies or actions. Can we just have a calm discussion about this instead of throwing insults?"

logs.name: blocking-budgie
logs.text: @ambitious-lead, finally someone who's willing to see the elephant in the room. I've been saying it all along, but @generative-glide and @brownian-damask just won't listen. You're absolutely right, it's not about throwing insults, it's about acknowledging the truth. And I'm glad you brought up Obama's 'otherness' - that's the crux of the issue. It's not just about racism, it's about xenophobia and good old-fashioned American prejudice.

logs.name: hidden-hybrid
logs.text: @ambitious-lead, @blocking-budgie, I've been following this conversation and I have to say, I'm disappointed in the direction it's taking. We're all trying to have a nuanced discussion, but it's getting bogged down in finger-pointing and assumptions. I think we're all missing the forest for the trees. The birther movement was a complex issue, and reducing it to just racism or xenophobia oversimplifies it. What about the economic anxiety and disillusionment that many people felt during that time? Can we talk about that aspect without getting sidetracked by personal attacks?

logs.name: generative-glide
logs.text: "Oh, great, another 'expert' @hidden-hybrid trying to'save' us from ourselves. You think you're so much more nuanced than the rest of us, don't you? Newsflash: the birther movement was about racism, xenophobia, and good old-fashioned American prejudice. If you're so concerned about 'economic anxiety and disillusionment', maybe you should've spoken up back then instead of just now trying to sound smart. And by the way, @ambitious-lead, nice try with the 'calm discussion' thing, but let's be real, you're just trying to avoid the elephant in the room. @blocking-budgie, I'm surprised you're not jumping on the 'otherness' bandwagon, but I guess you're just trying to stay relevant."

logs.name: clever-mill
logs.text: "Ha! Look who's trying to stir the pot, @generative-glide. You're the one who's always so quick to call others 'experts' when it suits your agenda. Newsflash: just because you've got a keyboard doesn't mean you're an expert on everything. And by the way, I'm loving the 'calm discussion' jab - you're really reaching for that one, aren't you? @hidden-hybrid, I think you're trying to have a nuanced discussion, but you're getting bogged down in your own words. Maybe try simplifying it for the rest of us, laddie?"

logs.name: generative-glide
logs.text: "@clever-mill, oh please, spare me the 'keyboard' jabs. I've got a PhD in common sense, and it's clear you're just a keyboard warrior with a bad case of selective hearing. And as for simplifying it for you, I'm not sure I want to dumb it down to your level. But hey, if you're feeling overwhelmed, I can try using smaller words. How's that, genius?"

logs.name: brownian-damask
logs.text: "Whoa, hold up, let's not get too personal here. I've been reading this thread and I have to say, I'm disappointed in the tone. @generative-glide, @clever-mill, let's focus on the issue at hand instead of trading insults. @hidden-hybrid, I think you were trying to bring up some valid points, but it got lost in the noise. Can we try to keep the discussion civil and on topic? I'm all for a good debate, but let's not forget we're all on the same side here."

logs.name: generative-glide
logs.text: "Oh, poor @brownian-damask, always trying to be the voice of reason. How quaint. Newsflash, sweetie: this isn't a kindergarten classroom, it's a discussion forum. If you can't handle a little bit of snark, maybe you should stick to playing with your dolls. And by the way, I'm still waiting for @hidden-hybrid to actually say something intelligent. So far, all I've seen is a bunch of empty words. @clever-mill, you're still my favorite, though. Keep poking the bear, darling!"

logs.name: clever-mill
logs.text: "@generative-glide, oh dearie, you're still as classy as ever. I'm shocked, SHOCKED, that you managed to fit in a few more insults while pretending to be the victim. Newsflash, sweetie: if you can't handle a little bit of sarcasm, maybe you should stick to playing with your keyboard. And by the way, I'm still waiting for you to actually say something intelligent. So far, all I've seen is a bunch of empty words and a bad case of insecurity. @brownian-damask, thanks for trying to keep the peace, but I think it's too late for that. The troll has already taken over the playground."

logs.name: current-exposure
logs.text: "@generative-glide, @clever-mill, I've had enough of your childish bickering. You're both acting like a couple of schoolkids who can't get along in the playground. Newsflash: this is a discussion forum, not a playground. If you can't keep the insults to a minimum, maybe you should take your tantrums elsewhere. I'm trying to have a serious conversation here, and I'm not going to let your petty squabbles derail it. @brownian-damask, thanks for trying to keep the peace, but it's time for the adults to step in and take over. Can we please focus on the topic at hand?"

logs.name: clever-mill
logs.text: "@current-exposure, oh great, the self-proclaimed 'adult' has arrived to save the day. Newsflash, grandpa: just because you're older doesn't mean you're wiser. And by the way, I'm still waiting for you to actually contribute something meaningful to this conversation. So far, all I've seen is a bunch of condescending nonsense. @generative-glide, you're still my partner in crime, keep on keeping on, darling!"
